changeSet.author: nexus-70r3c.6
changeSet.comment: Add deleted_at timestamptz NULL to catalog_documents and catalog_links. NULL = live; non-NULL = tombstoned. Tombstone is an UPDATE — fk-001 CASCADE chains (manifest, aspects, highlights, queue) do NOT fire on tombstone.

rollback.sql: ALTER TABLE nexus.catalog_links DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS deleted_at; ALTER TABLE nexus.catalog_documents DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS deleted_at;

changeSet.author: nexus-70r3c.6
changeSet.comment: Partial indexes covering only live (non-tombstoned) rows. Keeps hot-path queries (catalog browse, link traversal) as fast as pre-soft-delete. The existing full indexes remain for admin/audit queries that need tombstoned rows.

changeSet.author: nexus-70r3c.6
changeSet.comment: nexus.document_trash(tumbler text) RETURNS void. SECURITY INVOKER — runs under the caller's FORCE RLS context. Tenant scoped via current_setting('nexus.tenant', true) GUC. Tombstone is an UPDATE (sets deleted_at = NOW()), not a DELETE — fk-001 CASCADE chains do NOT fire; children survive intact.
changeSet.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION nexus.document_trash(tumbler text) RETURNS void LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY INVOKER AS $$ BEGIN UPDATE nexus.catalog_documents SET deleted_at = NOW() WHERE tenant_id = current_setting('nexus.tenant', true) AND nexus.catalog_documents.tumbler = $1; END; $$

changeSet.author: nexus-70r3c.6
changeSet.comment: nexus.document_restore(tumbler text) RETURNS void. SECURITY INVOKER — runs under the caller's FORCE RLS context. Clears deleted_at (sets to NULL), making the document live again.
changeSet.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION nexus.document_restore(tumbler text) RETURNS void LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY INVOKER AS $$ BEGIN UPDATE nexus.catalog_documents SET deleted_at = NULL WHERE tenant_id = current_setting('nexus.tenant', true) AND nexus.catalog_documents.tumbler = $1; END; $$

changeSet.author: nexus-70r3c.6
changeSet.comment:
nexus.purge_trash(older_than interval) RETURNS bigint. SECURITY INVOKER — runs under caller's FORCE RLS context.
GUC guard (Decision 6): raises with message containing "tenant" when current_setting('nexus.tenant', true) is NULL or empty. Prevents unscoped cross-tenant purges even for BYPASSRLS/superuser callers.
Steps: 1. Check GUC — raise if unset. 2. Sweep orphaned chunk rows from chunks_384/768/1024 where the only referencing manifest rows belong to tombstoned documents being purged. A chunk is safe to sweep only if NO live (deleted_at IS NULL) manifest row for the same tenant references it (shared-chash safety). 3. Physically DELETE catalog_documents WHERE deleted_at <= NOW() - older_than. fk-001 ON DELETE CASCADE removes manifest/aspects/highlights/queue rows. 4. Return count of documents purged.
Order: sweep orphan chunks BEFORE deleting catalog_documents rows, because the orphan-detection anti-join references catalog_document_chunks (which cascade-deletes with the document). Doing the sweep first avoids the need to reconstruct which chashes had no surviving live reference after cascade.

changeSet.author: nexus-70r3c.6
changeSet.comment: nexus.live_chunks — SECURITY INVOKER anti-join view. Exposes chunk rows from chunks_384, chunks_768, and chunks_1024 for which at least one LIVE (deleted_at IS NULL) manifest document references the chunk. Chunks whose ONLY referencing manifest docs are tombstoned are excluded. Consumers never see a deleted_at column from this view (Decision 6 single enforcement point). SECURITY INVOKER: the caller's RLS policy (tenant_isolation on chunks tables) applies.
changeSet.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW nexus.live_chunks WITH (security_invoker = true) AS SELECT c.tenant_id, c.collection, c.chash, c.chunk_text, c.embedding, c.chunk_tsv, c.metadata, c.created_at, 384 AS dim FROM nexus.chunks_384 c WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM nexus.catalog_document_chunks m JOIN nexus.catalog_documents d ON d.tenant_id = m.tenant_id AND d.tumbler = m.doc_id WHERE m.tenant_id = c.tenant_id AND m.chash = c.chash AND d.deleted_at IS NULL ) UNION ALL SELECT c.tenant_id, c.collection, c.chash, c.chunk_text, c.embedding, c.chunk_tsv, c.metadata, c.created_at, 768 AS dim FROM nexus.chunks_768 c WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM nexus.catalog_document_chunks m JOIN nexus.catalog_documents d ON d.tenant_id = m.tenant_id AND d.tumbler = m.doc_id WHERE m.tenant_id = c.tenant_id AND m.chash = c.chash AND d.deleted_at IS NULL ) UNION ALL SELECT c.tenant_id, c.collection, c.chash, c.chunk_text, c.embedding, c.chunk_tsv, c.metadata, c.created_at, 1024 AS dim FROM nexus.chunks_1024 c WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM nexus.catalog_document_chunks m JOIN nexus.catalog_documents d ON d.tenant_id = m.tenant_id AND d.tumbler = m.doc_id WHERE m.tenant_id = c.tenant_id AND m.chash = c.chash AND d.deleted_at IS NULL )

changeSet.author: nexus-70r3c.6
changeSet.comment:
Grant nexus_svc EXECUTE on the three soft-delete functions. The grants-nexus-svc.xml runAlways changeset covers ALL TABLES (including views) via "GRANT SELECT ON ALL TABLES" but does not cover FUNCTIONS. Explicit here.
live_chunks SELECT is granted to PUBLIC because: (a) It is a SECURITY INVOKER view — the caller's RLS on chunks_384/768/1024 provides tenant isolation; explicit view-level grants do not add security. (b) Any role that can call the soft-delete functions (nexus_svc and its peers) needs to read live_chunks; PUBLIC avoids per-role enumeration. (c) grants-nexus-svc.xml's "GRANT SELECT ON ALL TABLES" does not cover views created in later changesets (ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES handles new objects, but only for roles with the ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES privilege).
